column1: # EasyDE — Contrasts Definition File
# One row = one comparison to run across all specified strata (cell types
# tissues
# SEPARATOR: multi-value fields use "|" as delimiter.
#   Example:  "Sex|BMI|Age"  means three separate values: Sex
# COLUMN DOCUMENTATION:
#   contrast_id        Unique identifier for this comparison. Used in all output
#                      filenames and log messages. No spaces — use underscores.
#                      Example: T1D_vs_ND
#   contrast_var       Column name in sample_metadata.csv that holds the
#                      variable being tested. If using RuvSEQ
#                      correlates with the contrast_var is REMOVED from the final formula.
#                      This prevents accidentally regressing out disease signal.
#                      Example: disease_status
#   biosample_id_col   Column name in sample_metadata.csv that uniquely identifies
#                      each biosample — the donor or subject-level unit that DESeq2
#                      treats as one observation. In pseudobulk RNA-seq this is
#                      typically a donor ID
#                      Must exactly match column headers in the count matrices.
#                      NOTE: This is NOT the biological donor ID used for
#                      deduplication (see filtering.donor_col in config.yaml).
#                      A single donor may have multiple biosamples (e.g. two
#                      tissue types)
column2: but each biosample is one DESeq2 row.

column1: #                      Example: sample_accession
#   control_grp        Value in contrast_var that defines the REFERENCE group.
#                      Leave empty for numeric (continuous) contrast_var.
#                      Example: ND
#   experimental_grp   Value in contrast_var that defines the EXPERIMENTAL group.
#                      Leave empty for numeric (continuous) contrast_var.
#                      Example: T1D
#   covariates         Additional covariates included in the DESeq2 model formula.
#                      Separate multiple values with "|". Leave empty for none.
#                      Example: Sex|BMI
#   latent_corr_vars   Variables to correlate against RUVseq latent factors (W_k).
#                      All correlations are shown in the heatmap
#                      each latent factor captures.
#                      Should include the contrast_var itself plus known confounders.
#                      Separate multiple values with "|". Leave empty for none.
#                      Example: disease_status|Sex|BMI|Age
#   filter_col_1       Column name to filter samples on (first filter).
#                      Leave empty to apply no filters.
#                      Example: treatment
#   filter_val_1       Allowed value(s) for filter_col_1.
#                      Separate multiple allowed values with "|".
#                      Example: no_treatment
#   filter_col_2       Column name for a second filter (optional).
#   filter_val_2       Allowed values for filter_col_2.
#                      You can add as many filter_col_N / filter_val_N column
#                      PAIRS as you need (filter_col_3/filter_val_3
#                      The pipeline discovers all pairs automatically — just
#                      add columns and fill them in. Empty pairs are skipped.
#   strata             The biosample groups to loop the same analysis over.
#                      Each stratum gets its own independent run of the full
#                      analysis pipeline (filtering
#                      using only the biosamples belonging to that group.
#                      In practice this is almost always a list of cell types
#                      (e.g. from pseudobulk scRNA-seq) or tissue types (e.g.
#                      from bulk RNA-seq across multiple biopsy sites). It could
#                      also be organoid lines
#                      grouping layers
column2: or any other stratification that has its
